The â€śsuture-firstâ€ť technique allows suture to be passed independently through a desired amount of tissue prior to anchor implantation. Multiple suture configurations, including simple, cinch, and mattress stitches, can be accomplished with FiberWireÂ®, FiberLinkâ„˘, FiberStickâ„˘, and LabralTapeâ„˘ sutures using the Labral Scorpionâ„˘ or QuickPassâ„˘ SutureLassoâ„˘ suture retriever. Used like a labral PushLock anchor, this anchor offers all of the same knotless advantages (low-profile, quick, "suture- first" technique with multiple stitch configuration options) for surgeons who prefer a threaded anchor.
